things.name: Romsdal Museum
things.description: Open-air folk museum with over 50 historic buildings relocated from around the Romsdal region, including farmhouses, a stave church and maritime exhibits

things.name: Varden viewpoint
things.description: Iconic hilltop panorama 407m above Molde offering the legendary view of 87 snowcapped peaks across the fjord on clear days

things.name: Molde Cathedral
things.description: Norway's smallest cathedral from 1957, built to replace the original destroyed in WWII bombing, with fine stained glass and a peaceful memorial garden

things.name: Romsdalseggen Ridge
things.description: Norway's most famous ridge hike offering extraordinary views across the Romsdal valley from a jagged 10km route above Andalsnes

things.name: Molde Harbour
things.description: Picturesque harbour with fish restaurants, the coastal express Hurtigruten dock and views across Moldefjord to the snow-capped mountains

things.name: Bjornsonhuset
things.description: Cultural centre dedicated to Nobel laureate Bjornstjerne Bjornson, Norway's national poet, who spent significant time in the Romsdal region

things.name: Molde Jazz Festival
things.description: Scandinavia's oldest jazz festival held annually in July since 1961; Miles Davis performed here in 1964, and it remains a world-class event

things.name: Trollstigen Mountain Road
things.description: Norway's most dramatic mountain road with 11 hairpin bends climbing a 1:12 gradient; a National Scenic Route with waterfalls alongside

things.name: Hjertoya Island
things.description: Small island a short ferry ride from central Molde with a maritime museum, beaches and walking paths through an idyllic car-free environment

things.name: Aker Stadion
things.description: Home of Molde FK with a 11,800-seat capacity; one of Norway's most successful clubs and regular Europa League participants
